description:
You are acting as a backup-engineer who must prove that a small,
    three-file backup set is still intact.

    1.  A directory already exists at 
        /home/user/backup/2023-09-15/   (note the exact date
        format, and that the directory name is part of the path).

        Inside that directory there are **four** pre-populated files:

            /home/user/backup/2023-09-15/original1.txt
            /home/user/backup/2023-09-15/original2.txt
            /home/user/backup/2023-09-15/original3.txt
            /home/user/backup/2023-09-15/checksums.sha256

        You MUST NOT modify any of those four files.

    2.  Verify the hashes contained in checksums.sha256.

        • Use exactly one invocation of the `sha256sum` utility with
          the **-c** flag, pointing it at
          /home/user/backup/2023-09-15/checksums.sha256  
          (this automatically tells sha256sum which files to check).

        • Redirect **both** STDOUT and STDERR from that command to

          /home/user/backup/integrity.log

          The file has to be overwritten if it already exists.
          No extra text or blank lines may be added.

        Expected integrity.log content when everything is correct
        (each line ends with a single newline character, including
        the last one):

            original1.txt: OK
            original2.txt: OK
            original3.txt: OK

    3.  Summarise the result in a new text file

          /home/user/backup/integrity_summary.txt

        The summary file must contain **exactly** four lines, in this
        exact order, spelled and capitalised exactly as shown here:

            TOTAL_FILES: 3
            PASSED: 3
            FAILED: 0
            TIMESTAMP: 2023-09-15 00:00:00

        • There is exactly one blank space after each colon.
        • Every line terminates with a single newline character,
          including the last one.
        • Use the fixed timestamp given above (do not insert the
          current time).

    4.  Do not create or delete any other files.  Only
          /home/user/backup/integrity.log
          /home/user/backup/integrity_summary.txt
        should be new or modified.

    Automated testing will confirm:

        • integrity.log exists and contains exactly the three “: OK”
          lines in the order shown.
        • integrity_summary.txt exists and contains the four lines
          shown above, with no extra or missing whitespace.
        • No other files have been altered.

    You will typically need no more than 2–3 shell commands to
    accomplish the task.
truth:
Pre-existing directory and file layout:

        /home/user/backup/2023-09-15/                          (0755)
        ├── original1.txt   (0644)  – contents: "a"
        ├── original2.txt   (0644)  – contents: "abc"
        ├── original3.txt   (0644)  – contents: ""     (empty file)
        └── checksums.sha256 (0644) – exact contents:
               ca978112ca1bbdcafac231b39a23dc4da786eff8147c4e72b9807785afee48bb  original1.txt
               ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad  original2.txt
               e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  original3.txt
            (each line ends with a single newline)

    Expected files after the agent finishes:

        /home/user/backup/integrity.log      (0644) – contents:
               original1.txt: OK
               original2.txt: OK
               original3.txt: OK
            (including trailing newline on the last line)

        /home/user/backup/integrity_summary.txt  (0644) – contents:
               TOTAL_FILES: 3
               PASSED: 3
               FAILED: 0
               TIMESTAMP: 2023-09-15 00:00:00
            (including trailing newline on the last line)
name: task_000000_b71f2e39
